twitter_thread: Hit Windows Copilot's AI limits 7 times in 2 weeks, with 3 errors due to malformed input.
Bypassed API keys and billing by using the reverse engineered API to access GPT-4 and GPT-5 models.
Error handling is 80% of the work, with 20% of errors coming from API connectivity issues.
Postman and cURL have been instrumental in testing the API, with 50+ test cases run so far.
Invalid user input has caused 40% of errors, emphasizing the need for robust validation.
Automated 3 workflows, including document processing and data entry, with a 30% time savings.
What's the most creative way you've used Windows Copilot to automate a task? #aiforwindows #copilot
pack.linkedin: After two weeks with Windows Copilot, I've encountered AI limits 7 times, with 3 errors resulting from malformed input. Using the reverse engineered API, I've accessed GPT-4 and GPT-5 models without API keys or billing. Error handling accounts for 80% of the work, with 20% of errors stemming from API connectivity issues. Tools like Postman and cURL have facilitated testing, with over 50 test cases executed. Notably, 40% of errors have been caused by invalid user input, highlighting the importance of validation. I've automated 3 workflows, achieving a 30% time savings. What experiences have you had with automating tasks using Windows Copilot?
tiktok_hooks: 2 weeks, 7 AI limit hits, and 3 input errors with Windows Copilot
Reverse engineered API, bypassing API keys and billing for GPT-4 and GPT-5

pack.reddit_headline: Windows Copilot: 7 AI limit hits in 2 weeks, is it still worth using?
